text: Last class you read the obligation and liability clauses of the contract.
Today you learn to confirm the contract terms in a formal email.

I am writing to confirm the contract terms.
I would be grateful if you could confirm the effective date.
Please find enclosed the revised price table.
I look forward to working with you.
Hi Walyson. Further to our call, could you confirm the contract terms in writing today?
Of course. I am writing to confirm them now. I would be grateful if you could confirm the delivery week.
As per our agreement, it is the first week of March. Did you receive the revised price table?
Yes, I acknowledge receipt of it, and I accept the amendment to clause two. Please find enclosed the countersigned contract.
Perfect. Would you kindly add one line about the warranty? I await your email at your earliest convenience.
The warranty still covers the premium grade. I will send the email now, and I look forward to working with you.
Would you kindly countersign the contract?

Hi Walyson, this is Clara from the trading desk. I am sending you a quick voice note before my written email. Further to our last call about the coffee contract, I would be grateful if you could confirm three terms in writing. First, kindly confirm the effective date and the delivery week. Second, please find enclosed the revised price table, and let me know if you accept the amendment to clause two. Third, I need one clarification about the warranty: does it still cover the premium grade? If everything is correct, kindly countersign the contract and send it back at your earliest convenience. I will await your written reply, and I look forward to closing this deal with you. Thank you very much.

This is my note before I write the formal reply to Clara. My goal is to confirm the terms clearly and politely in writing. First, I will open with a formal greeting and write that I am writing to confirm the contract terms. Then I will confirm the effective date and the delivery week, exactly as she asked. I will acknowledge receipt of the revised price table and accept the amendment to clause two. I will give the clarification about the warranty: yes, it still covers the premium grade. After that, I will say that I enclose the countersigned contract. Finally, I will close with a polite sign-off and say that I look forward to working with her. I will proofread the email once before I send it.

First, open the email with a formal greeting and a clear subject line. Next, write one line that says why you are writing, for example I am writing to confirm the contract terms. After that, make your requests politely, using would you kindly or I would be grateful if you could. Then, confirm the terms the other side asked about and say please find enclosed for any document. Finally, close with a polite sign-off and a line that says you look forward to their reply, and proofread the whole email before you send it.
